# Repro: deleting a synthetic chord-interior vertex on a discretized
# arc. Sketch is a rect with one curved edge (the right one). After
# `discretize_polygon`, that arc edge becomes a chord chain with
# synthetic ids. The user's `DeleteVertex` annotation addresses the
# parent sketch edge by `(start_vid, end_vid)`; the engine should
# resolve to the nearest graph vertex on the chord chain (i.e. the
# synthetic vertex closest to t=0.5) and remove it.
#
# Bug: previously the annotation went dormant because (a)
# `world_to_perimeter_pos` projected onto the chord between sketch
# corners instead of the underlying arc, and (b) the engine resolver
# couldn't match the (start, end, t) target to any graph vertex.

polygon outer
0,0
240,0
arc 0.2
240,160
0,160
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

set stall-angle=90
----
set stall_angle_deg=90

# Sketch ids assigned by `ensure_ids` (engine-side, before
# discretization) are 1..4 in walk order: 1=(0,0), 2=(240,0),
# 3=(240,160) (post-arc), 4=(0,160). The arc edge runs 2→3.
# bulge=0.2 + the default `arc_discretize_tolerance=5` yields
# exactly 2 chords on the arc — one synthetic vertex at the
# chord-chain midpoint, hit precisely by t=0.5.
annotation delete-vertex on=perimeter loop=outer start=2 end=3 t=0.5
----
annotation delete-vertex on=perimeter loop=outer edge=2→3 t=0.50

generate
----
total_stalls: 57

# The annotation must NOT be dormant — that's the whole point.
dormant
----
dormant_annotations: 0
